steps.title: Isolate $j$
steps.content: Start with $$8j = k + 15m.$$ Since $j$ is multiplied by $8$, divide both sides by $8$ to solve for $j$.

steps.title: Divide both sides by 8
steps.content: $$j = \frac{k + 15m}{8}.$$ The entire right side, including both $k$ and $15m$, is divided by $8$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $j=\frac{k}{8}+15m$ — divides only $k$ by $8$, leaving $15m$ undivided. Incorrect.
  • Choice B: $j=k+\frac{15m}{8}$ — divides only $15m$ by $8$, leaving $k$ undivided. Incorrect.
  • Choice C: $j=8(k+15m)$ — multiplies by $8$ instead of dividing. Incorrect.
  • Choice D: $j=\frac{k+15m}{8}$ — correctly divides the whole right side by $8$. Correct.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
